reviews.review_msg: Seemed kind of pretentious at first, but explained everything really well and had well constructed power points (and he didn't just read from them). Questions encouraged

He is unclear about what to expect from his paper. He also expects u to remember an entire textbook of material for his final and its just way too much to remember for a first year course. He doesn't specify how the tutorial participation works and overall i wouldn't recommend this class. He doesnt seem to be kind with his grading either.

A really good course thanks to a professor who's passionate and (obviously) reeeeealy loves History. Taking his course in Surrey kind of sucked, because the campus set-up made it hard for him to have regular office hours, and the tutorials were kind of weird (strange fishbowl rooms), but Lanthier was inspiring in a quirky, cynical sort of way.

A year later, I realize that this course was both super-useful for taking more advance History AND that it was taught by somebody really nice who knows his stuff. The course seemed kind of hard at the time, but now I can see I was wrong. There was a lot of reading, but Dr. Lanthier's marks were really generous.

Michael is very kind and helpful. His lectures are great but his slides do not really give much information. For the most part, he reiterates the assigned textbook readings and just expands within his point of view in lectures. In tests and wants specificity but quite generous in essays. Overall great professor, but a tough marker.

Julian is very passionate about history and it shows in his lectures. If you approach him for help he is more than willing to give you a hand. He is a very understanding prof and listens to what you have to say. Would definitely recommend him if you are interested in History or need a Humanities elective!

Dr. Brooks was a pleasure to have this semester. HIST 130 is a massive class, so get ready to read tons. Because this class has so much material, it's better to have a midterm but Brooks didn't give one. He gives great feedback and is super knowledgeable and nice. Definitely recommend!

Dr. Ray is a kind person, however as a professor she would talk over students during lecture discussions, graded quite harshly, and viewed small mistakes as catastrophic. There were sample assignments and criteria for how assignments would be graded, but she marked as though she was looking for mistakes. Course content was interesting though.
She cancels last minute all the time, only received six in person lectures all semester. still hasn't graded stuff we submitted two months ago. Grading rubrics are super unclear.

Dr. Ray was quite approachable, and she explained our assignments in depth with multiple examples/templates. The course was super fun, as the content was quite interesting! The course work was also decent-- only 3 assignments, an academic journal, and participation. There was no midterm or final. I loved HIST-130, I recommend it to everyone!

Decent prof. This is by no means a gpa booster but you can get by if you read ahead and discuss the assignment details carefully. Overall easy course requirements wise but grading is slightly strict so give it ur all but in a smart way. Better confirm thing ahead rather than regretting later as they say

My fav teacher at SFU so far. Caring, sweet and engaging with students. She was my tutorial instructor as well and a joy to talk to. Very interesting assignments and very clear rubrics, with example assignments provided. Wasn't a fan of history, I now would love to take another class with her. Really cares for the students well beings and success.

Loveddd her, so sweet and caring. There were no quizzes or exams in this class, just written assignments. She's a very easy marker and gives good feedback. She was my TA in my tutorial as well and sparked some great conversations. Super nice, she feels like a role model.

Dr. Ray is an incredibly caring and attentive professor who genuinely cares about her fellow faculty and students. She was always very understanding and very easy on her students. I recommend taking her classes!

Very fun! Has great energy on a Monday, and the lack of quizzes/exams was a lifesaver. Is enthusiastic about helping with assignments and provides tons of valuable feedback. However, do NOT take this class if you're bad at/don't like writing. It is writing assignment-heavy, and minor mistakes will hurt your grade. Highly recommend HIST130!

My experience - Great prof overall, would love to have him again for another course - Lots of online weekly course readings, but manageable - Lectures were informative and engaging (they were also recorded and you could attend via zoom) - Unique points-based grading system involving various written assignments.

Clossey is a good prof and I think overall Hist 130 is a great class to take as an elective. The class has no exams, with a point system grading format. Lectures are recorded, while tutorials count towards participation points and overall grade. It's really citation-heavy and you really need to make sure you pass your assignments to do well.

Great professor, really shows that he cares about the subject material. Course uses an alternative grading system that allows you to know what your grade will be. Also NO TESTS AT ALL, grades are based on participation in tutorials and assignments. He's super easy to reach outside of class. Good elective for non history students

Very passionate and puts a lot of effort into the lectures. The assignments have clear grading criteria and it's easy to pass the class with a high grade as long as you do your assignments and show up to tutorial and participate in group discussions. Lots of tutorial readings, a new one each week, can vary between 10-30ish pages.

Dr. Clossey's class was one I quite enjoyed. His lectures are all over the place but quite entertaining, and I found the material covered in class and tutorial to be diverse and interesting. Do be warned, getting above a B+ is going to be challenging with how the assignments are structured, however, anything below an A- is extremely easy to get
Great teacher, can tell he loves history. Records his lectures so don't have to attend. Tuts have some participation marks.

This class was super easy, I only showed up to 2 lectures and got a B, as all the assignment materials are based on what we talk about in tutorial. There is no tests. That being said, it's important to show up to tutorial and participate as it's worth a good percentage of your grade. Content was super interesting and would 100% take again.

Luke Clossey is a great prof! I took his HIST130, and it was very easy for me to get a B without much effort. I showed up to the tutorials and participated; I would recommend preparing just a little bit so you can make sure you get participation marks. No midterms and no finals, all you need to do are six intakes (essays), which were fairly easy.

- Very cool course if you like the more general kind of history.
- You hardly saw the professor since the class is just one tutorial a week
- Not too much course material or work
- Relatively easy to get a good grade if you follow all the instructions.
- Kinda relies on having a good TA though...

At first, I thought his grading criteria to be quite unfair (which it kind of is), but if you follow the guidelines to a T, you'll have no problem doing well on the papers. Lectures are quite interesting, and he is an emphatic speaker. Just don't take a class with him if you aren't a strong writer, as his rubric heavily chastises poor writing.
There are no quizzes, midterms, and there is no final. Grade is based on the number of assignments passed and participation.

Prof Malik was a great lecturer who made all of the information accessible in lectures and explained difficult concepts. She gave great feedback on our assignments and showed care to her students. Her lectures had a lot of information and were not recorded, so make sure you go. She gives all the readings in canvas, so no textbook is needed.

She's really friendly and nice. Your mark will be based on a few papers but from my experiance if you put in effort, do your readings and don't plagiarize you'll be fine. Overall I would definitely recommend taking this course
